mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or t-stat of mean return > 0 during trigger periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP growth and moderate consumer price inflation indicates robust aggregate demand without triggering aggressive monetary tightening, creating a favorable environment for positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive GDP and CPI signals yield negative median 60-day returns, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which signals immediate corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat when INDPRO accelerates, or rolling 1-year IC drops below 0.01 during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in retail sales indicates immediate weakness in consumer demand, which reduces near-term corporate earnings forecasts and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in > 60% of trigger events, or signal loses predictive power when inflation exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show negative mean returns during payroll growth periods, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates combined with accelerating consumer price inflation signal a tightening monetary policy response to overheating demand, which increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields positive median 20-day returns, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production indicate a contraction in manufacturing output and weakening business investment, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 0.5% MoM for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 70% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction when global trade volumes are expanding.
